name: Boltz-2
description: Predicts the 3D structure of proteins, rna, dna and small molecules. Handles modified residues, covalent ligands and glycans, as well as condition the generation on pocket residues. Matches or outperforms AlphaFold3. Predict binding affinity given a binder chain.
capabilities: Predicts the 3D structure of proteins, rna, dna and small molecules
Handles modified residues, covalent ligands and glycans, as well as condition the generation on pocket residues
Predict binding affinity given a binder chain

sequences.description: Array of entity objects. Each entity has a type (protein, rna, dna, or ligand) and corresponding sequence or SMILES.

sequence.description: Amino acid sequence for protein, nucleotide sequence for RNA/DNA

ccd.description: CCD code for ligand entities as alternative to SMILES

numSamples.description: Number of structure predictions to return

numRecycles.description: Number of rounds of refinement during structure prediction

stepScale.description: Lower step scale means more diversity. Recommended between 1 and 2.

numSeeds.description: Number of random seeds for sampling

useMSA.description: Whether to use multiple sequence alignment

customA3mFiles.description: Custom multiple sequence alignment files for protein chains

maxMsaSequences.description: Maximum number of sequences to use for MSA

templateCifs.description: Template CIF files to guide structure prediction

templateDistanceThreshold.description: Distance in Angstroms that the prediction can deviate from the template

usePotentials.description: Whether to run the Boltz-2 model using inference time potentials

contactRestraints.description: Contact restraints between two residues specifying maximum distances

pocketRestraints.description: Pocket restraints between a residue and a chain

generateIpsaeMetrics.description: Generate IPSAE metrics for protein-protein complex interfaces
